response.justification: Candidate has directly relevant current experience building MCP code generators and agentic developer tooling at re:cinq, plus solid distributed-systems exposure from Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec). However, the resume does not list Python anywhere, which is a hard must-have for this role, and most of the heavy-throughput work was in DLang rather than Python. Worth a screen to verify Python depth and clarify Staff-level scope; if Python is hands-on, this becomes a stronger yes.
strengths: Current hands-on work on MCP and AI-agent infrastructure aligns with the AI-native engineering baseline
Real distributed/high-throughput background (Sociomantic real-time bidding scale)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js plus craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based, CI/CD)
concerns: Python not listed in skills despite being a must-have for the role
Resume describes contributions more than Staff-level technical strategy or scale-of-influence
No explicit evidence of on-call rotation ownership or large-scale stakeholder/cross-functional leadership
